1999-09-01.title: Congo Rebels Finally Sign an Accord but Real Peace Is Still Distant
1999-09-01.abstract: Divided rebels in Congo sign cease-fire accord signed by Rwanda, Uganda, Zimbabwe, Angola, Namibia and Congo month ago, Lusaka, Zambia; continue to squabble about how to carry out accord (M)

1999-09-01.title: New Tremor Shakes Quake-Devastated Area of Turkey
1999-09-01.abstract: Strong tremor and aftershock hit already earthquake-devastated towns in northwestern Turkey, bringing down several buildings and causing at least one death; 166 people are admitted to hospitals and clinics in Izmit after being hit by debris; tremor is felt in some parts of Istanbul, where main effect is psychological; prosecutors in Duzce seek arrest of 33 contractors, engineers and building owners in connection with fatal building collapses; maps; photo (M)

1999-09-01.title: A Palace Fit for a Fugitive And Ukraine's Ex-Premier
1999-09-01.abstract: Ukrainian ex-Prime Min Pavlo Lazarenko, detained by United States for possible extradition to Ukraine or Switzerland on charges of embezzling state funds and money laundering, bought lavish $6.75 million Marin County, Calif, estate for cash last Sept; US authorities say he made money buying and selling natural gas contracts when he was energy minister; his attorney holds charges are politically motivated; photos (M)

1999-09-01.title: Argentine Jet Crashes on Takeoff; as Many as 80 Feared Dead
1999-09-01.abstract: LAPA flight carrying some 106 people crashes on takeoff from Buenos Aires, Argentina, and catches fire; as many as 80 people are killed and at least 23 hurt, including people on ground; map (M)

1999-09-01.title: Militias Raise Tensions After Timor Votes
1999-09-01.abstract: Anti-independence thugs who have terrorized East Timor for months reassert selves after extraordinarily peaceful referendum on region's future in which 98.6 percent of voters reportedly cast ballots; thugs, mobilized by Indonesian military into irregular militias, set up roadblocks in Dili and on rural roads; Eurico Guterres, militia leader, says his men will bar what he calls East Timorese elite from leaving; some promoters of independence go into hiding after reportedly getting death threats; photo (M)

1999-09-02.title: Remembering Their War, Germans Embrace the First Victims
1999-09-02.abstract: Germany marks 60th anniversary of its invasion of Poland and start of World War II by declaring Poland should have place in European Union as soon as possible; Pres Johannes Rau visits Gdansk; most major German newspapers ignore anniversary; Chancellor Gerhard Schroeder's position, acknowledgment and awareness of past allied to determination to look beyond it, reflects that of large majority of Germans (M)

1999-09-02.title: U.S. Seeks Details on Russia's Use of Aid
1999-09-02.abstract: Clinton Administration officials say United States wants assurances that international financial aid to Russia has not been lost to corruption or moved surreptitiously out of nation; stop short of calling for halt in future International Monetary Fund loans; respond to reported money laundering through Bank of New York (M)

1999-09-02.title: Brinkmanship, Still, In the Mideast Talks
1999-09-02.abstract: Talks between Israelis and Palestinians stalled again early today and it seemed unlikely that new agreements would be reached before Secretary of State Madeleine Albright arrives later today.     State Department officials have said Ms. Albright would be reluctant to mediate the fine print, a key aspect of which involves the release of Arab prisoners by Israel.

1999-09-02.title: Justice Dept. Inquiry Into Sales Of Visas in Panama Is Denied
1999-09-02.abstract: United States officials say privately that while Panamanian officials probably are selling Panamanian visas to Chinese immigrants using Panama as back door to sneak into United States, Justice Department cannot open probe without indications that Panamanians also help Chinese to enter US; say consular officials acted properly in revoking visa of Samantha Smith, former head of Panamanian intelligence, who says she approved suspect visas at request of then-Pres Ernesto Perez Balladares; she claimed he sold visas to supplement budget; revocation drew anger of Justice officials, who say move tipped off smuggling ring; officials say Amb Simon Ferro kept all necessary agencies informed about actions; Sen Jesse Helms asks State and Justice Departments about supposed probe (M)

1999-09-02.title: Serbs Driven From Kosovo Live Crowded and in Want
1999-09-02.abstract: Kursumlija, Serbia, is struggling to absorb 7,000 displaced Serbs from Kosovo as Serbia deals with some 173,000 Kosovo refugees; most Kosovo Serbs get 60 percent of their regular salaries from state suffering severe cash shortage; many are crowded in with host families that cannot afford added burden; Serbia is no longer pressing them to return to Kosovo, but encourages them to live near province and discourages them from trying to settle in Belgrade, where they would be more visible; photo (M)

1999-09-03.title: MIDEAST STANDOFF REMAINS UNSOLVED AS ALBRIGHT VISITS
1999-09-03.abstract: Israelis and Palestinians fail to deliver agreement for signing ceremony in Egypt that Secretary of State Madeleine K Albright had hoped to preside over; Albright meets with Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat in Egypt, then arrives in Israel to meet with Prime Min Ehud Barak, but talks break up without a deal; at issue is Israel's refusal to release 50 Palestinian prisoners now held in Israel; negotiating atmosphere between Israelis and Palestinians was supposed to have substantially improved with new Israeli leader, Barak; it is not clear whether dispute over prisoners constitutes real crisis or matter of brinkmanship, with each side seeking to show its constituency that it is reaching for best possible bargain on emotional issue; photo (M)

1999-09-03.title: NATO and U.N. in Kosovo Agree On K.L.A. Role in Civilian Force
1999-09-03.abstract: NATO and United Nations officials agree to allow part of Kosovo Liberation Army to survive as lightly armed civilian emergency force; under Kosovo peace accord, rebel army is being dismantled this month under UN supervision; its successor, called Kosovo Corps, is to be civilian force, set up for coping with emergencies like earthquakes and fires; officials say its 3,000 members will have military structure formed from core commanders and brigades of present KLA; sorting out duties of new force remains extremely delicate, because of implications for Kosovo Albanians' aspiration for independence and for Yugoslavia's sovereignty over province; while NATO sees Kosovo Corps as civilian force, along lines of National Guard in US, rebel officers see it as potential core of national army, and are selling it to their followers as such (M)

1999-09-03.title: Turkey's Bereft Carry Cynicism Back to Villages
1999-09-03.abstract: Thousands of people who have lived in prosperous parts of Turkey are likely to return to poorer regions in wake of earthquake that devastated country's booming industrial region; reverse migration could have profound social and political effects; many of those returning to undeveloped areas will bring urban attitudes with them, along with new-found cynicism about their political system stemming from Government's failures in earthquake relief effort; if enough of these people fan out through rural Turkey, they may emerge as new force for social and political change; photo; maps (M)

1999-09-03.title: Bank of New York Dismisses Second Employee in Laundering Case
1999-09-03.abstract: Bank of New York says it has dismissed second employee involved in its investigation into possible money laundering at bank; employee, Svetlana Kudryavtsev, reportedly failed to cooperate with bank's internal probe of matter; bank earlier discharged Lucy Edwards for failing to cooperate with investigators and not revealing to bank that she was married to Peter Berlin, whose accounts at bank were allegedly used to channel billions of dollars from Russia; investigators are also looking at another bank officer, Natasha Gurfinkel Kagalovsky, who worked closely with Edwards and is married to prominent Russian businessman (M)

1999-09-03.title: Ariel Sharon, Resilient Hawk, Captures Likud Leadership
1999-09-03.abstract: Ariel Sharon captures leadership of opposition Likud Party in Israel, succeding Benjamin Netanyahu, who quit after losing bid to be re-elected Prime Minister in May; Sharon, 71, is veteran of many Israeli military and political battles; says he will serve only as steward of party, with goal of holding new primaries in two years to select next Likud candidate for Prime Minister; photo (M)

1999-09-04.title: State Election a Test for Germany's Governing Left
1999-09-04.abstract: State election in Saarland represents identity crisis for Germany's Social Democrat as Chancellor Gerhard Schroder's tries to reduce party's role as champion of workers' rights and make it promote a new entrepreneurial spirit; Schroder warily supports re-election of Saarland Governor, Reinhard Klimmt, who is a more traditional Social Democratic and faces fierce challenge from Christian Deemocatic candidate, Peter Muller, who has declared that Saarland coal industry, which employs 11,000 people, must be closed; photo; map (M)

1999-09-04.title: Swiss Freeze 59 Bank Accounts For Russian Corruption Inquiry
1999-09-04.abstract: Swiss prosecutors investigating suspected money laundering by officials at high levels of Russian Government order Swiss banks to provide information on two dozen Russians who may have accounts in Switzerland; freeze at least 59 bank accounts; Russians under investigation include Pavel P Borodin, his wife, daughter and son-in-law, and Oleg Soskovets; Swiss investigation was opened in response to requests made in spring by Russia's chief prosecutor, Yuri I Skuratov, who was subsequently fired by Pres Boris Yeltsin but remains technically in office because Russian Parliament has twice rejected his dismissal; photo (M)

1999-09-04.title: At Stake Is His Ability to Maintain Authority and Push Reform : Schroeder Facing Nasty Months
1999-09-04.abstract: SAARBRUECKEN, Germany—  Out here in what's left of blue-collarland, Gerhard Schroeder, the Brioni chancellor, comes to town to plead he's not really one of the guys in sharp suits who closed the mines and the mills, but a friend whose heart still thumps with Social Democratic compassion.

1999-09-04.title: A Meeting of Francophones, With Insults in Plain English
1999-09-04.abstract: Leaders from 52 countries with French-speaking populations gather in Moncton, New Brunswick, traditional capital of Acadia, and Canada's English press makes much of the fact that a good number of these leaders are among the world's worst human rights violators; Prime Min Jean Chretien addresses summit, called La Francophonie, and says it should give itself the power to purge the undemocratic; photo (M)

1999-09-04.title: Malula Journal; Passing On History by Word of Mouth
1999-09-04.abstract: Aramaic, the language of Jesus, survives as language of daily life of Malula, Syrian town of 5,000, and two nearby villages; successive conquerors never bothered to force their languages on this remote community in Qalamum Mountains; scholars visit Malula to study language; photos; map (M)

1999-09-05.title: Argentine Chief's Tepid Stand Hobbles His Party's Candidate
In Mideast, Wariness Mixed With New Hope
Assad Stands Firm on Conditions for Restarting Talks
1999-09-05.abstract: State Sec Madeleine K Albright meets in Syria with Pres Hafez al-Assad; Clinton Administration aide says meeting will not lead to immediate resumption of Israeli-Syrian peace talks, but lays groundwork for new talks; says Assad sticks to demand that Israel recognize 1967 border before talks resume; Albright visits Beirut, Lebanon, in effort to show that time is right to widen peace effort to include Syria and Lebanon and that Lebanese Government makes strides in normalizing daily life; meets with Prime Min Selim Hoss and calls for resumption of Israeli-Lebanese talks; photo (M)

1999-09-05.title: Venezuelans Applaud Leader's Assault on System
1999-09-05.abstract: Venezuelans have largely hailed moves by Constitutional Assembly loyal to Pres Hugo Chavez to strip opposition-controlled Congress of all its powers and take control of judicial system; Chavez's popularity has risen as result of his drive to eradicate establish order, drive deplored abroad as threat to democracy; sentiment has been building for at least decade against Congress and other state institutions seen as remote and unresponsive; photo (M)

1999-09-05.title: In Russia, a Power Play Acted Out on Television
1999-09-05.abstract: Russian television channel controlled by Boris A Berezovsky, ally of Pres Boris N Yeltsin, plans weekly news program whose host, Sergei Dorenko, is known for attacks on political opponents; program will be aired at same time as long-running weekly program hosted by Yevgeny Kiselyov on channel controlled by Vladimir Gusinsky, who has recently broken with Yeltsin's dwindling team; head-to-head competition is latest skirmish in media war linked to political power struggle; Dorenko recalls both channels scheduled their news programs to avoid competition when both backed Yeltsin, interview; photo (M)

1999-09-06.title: China Puts More Power in Pockets of the People
1999-09-06.abstract: Chinese Government announces sweeping increases in salaries, pensions and welfare payments for 84 million people, describing move as a birthday gift as the country prepares to celebrate 50th anniversary of founding of People's Republic; officials clearly hope raises will help their as yet futile attacks on stubborn deflation that has plagued China in its current economic slump (M)

1999-09-06.title: Schroder's Party Is Set Back Painfully in 2 State Elections
1999-09-06.abstract: Chancellor Gerhard Schroder of Germany suffers rebuff in state elections in Saarland and Brandenburg; in Saarland, Christian Democrats edge out Social Democrats, who have been in power for 15 years, and Peter Muller will take over state premiership from Reinhard Klimmt; far right Deutsche Volksunion takes 5.3 percent of vote and will qualify for parliamentary representation; in Brandenburg, Christian Democrats and Party of Democratic Socialism, which is the former East German Communist Party, each benefit from collapse of Social Democrats; photos; map (M)

1999-09-06.title: Canada's Other French-Speakers Cope
1999-09-06.abstract: Pres Jacques Chirac of France visits Memramcook, New Brunswick, and pledges to renew French support for Acadian people of province; Canada has made Moncton, New Brunswick, site of conference of leaders from 49 foreign nations with French-speaking populations and paints province as a place where French-speakers do not dream of secession but live happily inside federation; Acadians bask in their modern day cultural and economic renaissance; photo; map (M)

1999-09-06.title: Rebels Attack Caucasus Area; Bomb Kills 30
1999-09-06.abstract: Islamic militants mount ferocious assault in Russian republic of Dagestan, and a powerful bomb demolishes an apartment building housing Russian officers and their families in Buinaksk, republic's second-largest city; at least 30 people are killed and about 110 wounded in explosion; renewed fighting is unwelcome political test for Vladimir V Putin, Russia's new Prime Minister; photo; map (M)

1999-09-06.title: 2 Cars Packed With Explosives Blow Up In Israel, Killing 3 Suspected Terrorists
1999-09-06.abstract: Less than 24 hours after a new Israeli-Palestinian agreement was signed, two cars packed with explosives blew up almost simultaneously in two northern Israeli cities today.    In Tiberias and Haifa, the cars' occupants, presumed to be the unsuccessful bombers, were killed and several passers-by were wounded, one seriously.

1999-09-07.title: Vicus Journal; Children at Play Exorcise a Brutal War's Demons
1999-09-07.abstract: Center for Popular Promotion and Development, private Peruvian group, is using money from Peruvian Govt and two Dutch foundations in pilot project to relieve pain of children in Ayacucho Province who have been traumatized by Shining Patch guerrilla insurgency; social workers in Vicus and other villages are encouraging children to express joys and fears in friendly classroom setting, where they play mock war games that include guerrilla attacks on villages and successful village militia attacks and army counterattacks; photo; maps (M)

1999-09-07.title: Mitchell Says It's Up to Ulster Political Leaders to Settle Their Feud
1999-09-07.abstract: Former US Sen George J Mitchell, who helped broker 1998 Ulster accords, returns to Belfast, Northern Ireland, in effort to break stalemated peace talks between Protestant leaders and Irish Republican Army; says ending impasse is up to feuding political leaders, but that stalemate can be broken; urges political leaders to seize 'best' political opportunity in many years to set province on path to enduring peace and political stability; says he will not renegotiate original settlement, but will focus tightly on disarmament methods and timing and on creating executive Cabinet with members from rival parties to run new Northern Ireland Assembly, power-sharing institution at heart of agreement; impasse centers on disarming Irish Republican Army before assembly begins functioning (M)

1999-09-07.title: Israeli Arabs Suspected In Car Blasts, Police Hint
1999-09-07.abstract: Israeli police hold five people in connection with car bombings in Tiberias and Haifa on September 5; indicate suspects were Israeli Arabs; three would-be bombers were killed in blasts; photo (M)

1999-09-07.title: Thousands in East Timor Fleeing as Militias Attack
1999-09-07.abstract: Militias backed by Indonesian military, in apparently planned campaign of savagery, continue attacks on people of East Timor who overhwelmingly voted for independence; burn homes, killing residents and forcing many thousand to flee for safety; burn home of Roman Catholic Archbishop Carlos Belo in Dili, evicting 6,000 people taking refuge there and forcing Belo to flee; fire on Australian Consulate; major operation appears under way to force many thousands of people to flee into West Timor, which is part of Indonesia; Indonesian Pres B J Habibie declares martial law in East Timor; Govt releases Jose Alexandre Gusmao, rebel leader arrested seven years ago and is regarded as potential leader of new East Timorese nation; he is turned over to UN officials in Jakarta; says he will do everything in his power to bring peace to East Timor; maps; photo (M)

1999-09-07.title: Russians Kill 3 Serbs in Kosovo Skirmish; Win Praise From NATO
1999-09-07.abstract: Russian peacekeeping troops fatally shoot three armed Serbs in eastern Kosovo; come under fire after surprising Serbs who had ambushed car killing passenger; Serbs were also beating two wounded Albanians; NATO Sec Gen Javier Solana praises 3,000 Russian peacekeepers in Kosovo; says incident is proof of their professionalism, and that they deserve NATO support (M)

1999-09-08.title: The Timor Enigma
1999-09-08.abstract: News analysis of political turmoil in East Timor; crisis comes against backdrop of broader crisis of leadership in Indonesia, in which central Govt is weak and fragmented as politicians fight over presidential succession and once-powerful military has become hestiant, angry and divided; while it is clear that irregular militias backed by military are carrying out campaign of terror, less clear is what campaign is meant to achieve and what degree of control Govt in Jakarta has over people waging it; photo (M)

1999-09-08.title: Yeltsin Derides His Military For Dagestan 'Carelessness'
1999-09-08.abstract: Pres Boris Yeltsin derides Russian military's handling of Islamic rebellion in Dagestan as inexcusably careless, joining chorus of critics who say nation's commanders appear to have learned nothing from humiliating defeat by rebels in Chechnya three years ago (M)

1999-09-08.title: U.N. Says a Quarter of East Timorese Have Fled
1999-09-08.abstract: Spokesman for UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan reports quarter of East Timor's population has been driven from homes in past four days by militias opposed to independence; says many displaced people who sought refuge in churches and aid agencies are being rounded up and forcibly moved across border to Indonesian West Timor; says thousands of others have fled into hills and forests; UN officials in East Timor say they face emergency with potential catastrophic consequences; most foreigners have been evacuated; remaining 335 UN staff members are trapped in compound in capital of Dili; virtually all relief aid has ended; almost no food is available for emergency distribution and fate of many refugees remains unknown; Security Council gives Indonesian Pres B J Habibie and military commander Gen Wiranto at least 24 more hours to end reign of terror seemingly orchestrated by units of military; East Timor's Roman Catholic Archbishop Carlos Ximenes Belo seeks refuge in Darwin, Australia; was driven from country by armed militias (M)

1999-09-08.title: Indonesia Is Warned It Could Lose Western Aid
1999-09-08.abstract: Clinton Administration and International Monetary Fund separately warn Indonesia that financial aid that has kept country economically afloat for last two years could be suspended if violence in East Timor is not brought under control (M)

1999-09-08.title: Of Bombs and Israeli Arabs, and Years of Loyalty
1999-09-08.abstract: Deaths of three Israeli Arabs in two car bomb explosions in Hafia and Tiberias on September 5 send shock waves through Israeli Arab communities; perhaps first suicide car-bombing attempt by Israeli Arabs has horrified many Arabs in small community of Mashad, who fear they may all be tarred with same brush as Palestinian militants from West Bank and Gaza; photos; map (M)

1999-09-09.title: With Other Goals in Indonesia, U.S. Moves Gently on East Timor
1999-09-09.abstract: United States, seeking to preserve its relationship with Indonesia, is resisting direct threats of economic or military sanctions as Clinton Administration protests chaos in East Timor; Defense Department, which has longstanding ties with Indonesian military, is taking lead in dealing with crisis; Joint Chiefs of Staff chairman, Gen Henry H Shelton, has discussed by phone with Gen Wiranto plan to remove troops from East Timor that have allied selves with anti-independence militias and replace them with soldiers loyal to central Government (M)

1999-09-09.title: Accusations of Bribery In the Kremlin Mount Up
1999-09-09.abstract: Felipe Turover, who worked for Swiss Banca del Gottardo as debt collector in Russia, says he saw credit card bills and photocopies of credit cards bearing signatures of Pres Boris N Yeltsin and his daughters that were provided by Swiss construction company Mabetex Project Engineering, interview; says bank officials described to him Kremlin meeting at which Pavel Borodin and other senior Russian officials accepted money and gifts from Mabetex, which did $1.5 billion worth of work for Kremlin; scores Yeltsin for giving Russia over to corruption and decline; is key figure in Swiss probe; photo (M)

1999-09-09.title: Calcutta Journal; Intellectuals' Pursuit: Putting English in Its Place
1999-09-09.abstract: Determined clique of Bengali intellectuals is pressing for change in official English spelling of Calcutta to Kolkata, as West Bengal city's name is pronounced in Bengali language; changing of municipal names has become matter of post-colonial pride in India; new names are slowly overtaking old in common usage; map (M)

1999-09-09.title: World Briefing
1999-09-09.abstract: EUROPE    NETHERLANDS: KEY JUDGE QUITS WAR PANEL -- One of the most senior judges of the Yugoslav war crimes tribunal, Antonio Cassese, will resign next February, the third key figure to quit the United Nations court in recent months. Judge Cassese, an Italian, who has served on the court since its creation in 1993, said he would go back to being a law professor at Florence University, nearly two years before the end of his term. Gabrielle Kirk McDonald, the tribunal president, will leave in November and Louise Arbour, the chief prosecutor, steps down on Sept. 15.   Marlise Simons (NYT)

1999-09-09.title: As Support for Intervention Grows, Indonesia Persuades U.N. to Delay Evacuation of Mission
1999-09-09.abstract: United Nations Sec Gen Kofi Annan and Security Council back away from confrontation with Indonesia after Pres B J Habibie and Gen Wiranto beg for more time to quell East Timor violence; Annan suspends for day plan to evacuate UN mission; Security Council, meeting privately, rejects Portugal's plea for open debate; Wiranto, who replaces his commander in East Timor, reportedly has little control in area, which is controlled by officers linked to ex-Pres Suharto's son-in-law, Lt Gen Prabowo Subianto (M)

1999-09-10.title: U.S. Report Details World's Religious Persecution
1999-09-10.abstract: United States State Dept report finds that while number of freely elected governments continues to grow throughout world, there are still many countries in which people face harassment, imprisonment or even death for practicing their religion; dept's first annual Report on International Religious Freedom cites Afghanistan, China, Iran, Iraq, Saudi Arabia and Sudan as among most repressive countries (M)

1999-09-10.title: Indian Courts World's Riches and Poor's Vote
1999-09-10.abstract: N Chandrababu Naidu, cyber-savvy Chief Minister of Andhra Pradesh and India's most dynamic economic reformer, wages his campaign for Parliament not by talking about economic growth, but by speaking traditional language of social programs: marriage halls for Muslims, irrigation canals for farmers, free hand tools for artisans, matching grants to poor women who save money; politicians across India are watching to see whether Naidu's aggressive blend of old-style politicking and new-wave economics gives his party a victory when votes for parliamentary elections are counted on Oct 6; as India's states become more increasingly important in drive for economic growth that is needed to pull hundreds of millions of Indians out of poverty, state leaders like Naidu are at vanguard of efforts to modernize local economies and integrate them into global marketplace; photo; map (L)

1999-09-10.title: In Clinton's Words: East Timor, Waco, Clemency Offer, and Other Issues
1999-09-10.abstract: Excerpts from Pres Clinton's statement on East Timor; photo (M)

1999-09-10.title: APEC Countries Defer to UN on East Timor
1999-09-10.abstract: AUCKLAND, New Zealand—  A meeting of 20 nations on the crisis in East Timor urged Indonesia on Thursday to end the violence in the disputed territory, but stopped short of taking any concrete action.

1999-09-10.title: U.S. General Was Overruled in Kosovo
1999-09-10.abstract: Gen Henry H Shelton, Chmn of Joint Chiefs of Staff, discloses that British general in charge of NATO forces in Kosovo, Gen Sir Michael Jackson, refused to obey an order from NATO's supreme commander, Gen Wesley K Clark, an American, to evict Russian troops after their surprise takeover of main airfield there in June; dispute between two commanders was resolved quickly by British and Ameican military leaders in favor of Jackson; Jackson had rejected order from his superior by saying 'No, I'm not going to do that. It's not worth starting World War III'; dispute raises doubts about NATO's chain of command (M)

1999-09-11.title: Afghanistan Opium Record Raises U.N. Fears
1999-09-11.abstract: United Nations report claims Afghanistan has more than doubled its production of opium in past year and now accounts for three-quarters of world opium crop; jump in production alarms head of United Nations Office for Drug Control and Crime Prevention, Pino Arlacchi; Arlacchi says he told Secretary Gen Kofi Annan that Afghan opium production is spinning out of control; fears Afghan output could flood Western Europe, primary market for heroin refined from Afghan opium (M)

1999-09-11.title: xxx: Crusading Cleveland Suburb Bars Cell-Phoning Drivers
1999-09-11.abstract: A growing number of countries have made it a crime to drive while speaking on a cellular phone, citing evidence that the distraction can pose a serious risk. But in the United States such bans are still limited to one small suburb of Cleveland, Ohio.

1999-09-11.title: Indonesia Assisting Militias, U.S. Says
1999-09-11.abstract: Pres Clinton accuses Indonesian military of direct involvement in savagery that has swept across East Timor and says international peacekeeping force must be allowed to restore order; Pentagon has insisted that there are no plans to send American ground troops to join Australian-led peacekeeping force in East Timor, but now Clinton's security adviser, Samuel R Berger, says nothing has been ruled out; Berger appears to acknowledge that Indonesia's military has muscled aside Pres B J Habibie, who seems unable to control developments in East Timor (M)

1999-09-11.title: Russian Promises Help From Police
1999-09-11.abstract: Foreign Min Igor S Ivanov of Russia says team of Russian law enforcement officials is going to Washington in effort to prove Moscow's eagerness to cooperate with American inquiries into money laundering; aide to Secretary of State Madeline K Albright says Russian corruption and its effect on international financial system was high on agenda during her recent meeting with Ivanov (S)

1999-09-11.title: Grabouw Journal; Reality Check for Anti-crime Trekker
1999-09-11.abstract: Roger Russell, former miner who is on 2,800-mile walk around South Africa to call attention to nation's notorious crime rate, is himself mugged; this underscores fact that crime has become one of biggest problems in South Africa and issue new Government of Pres Thabo Mbeki is eager to make a priority; Russell decided on his walk against crime after seeing videotape of South African police officers beating up suspect; his walk has two aims: as general protest against crime and to support police officers who are not engaged in brutality; photo; map (M)

1999-09-12.title: CLINTON AND JIANG HEAL RIFT AND SET NEW TRADE COURSE
1999-09-12.abstract: Pres Clinton and China Pres Jiang Zemin, in Auckland, New Zealand, for Asian economic summit conference, declare end to months of frozen relations following bombing of Chinese Embassy in Belgrade, and their trade ministers begin meeting in hopes of closing deal that will get China into World Trade Organization this year; Chinese officials are pleased that Clinton has been critical of Taiwan Pres Lee Teng Hui for his statements describing island as separate state (M)

1999-09-12.title: To Appease U.S., Kazakh Acts on MIG Sales
1999-09-12.abstract: Kazakhstan Pres Nursultan A Nazarbayev, with strategic ties to US at risk over sale of MIG jet fighters to North Korea, orders series of arrests, reforms government's arms export system and dismisses his closest adviser,, Nurtai Abukayev (M)

1999-09-12.title: Nigeria at Dawn of a Democracy Finds Making Laws Is No Picnic
1999-09-12.abstract: Nigeria has ended nearly 16 years of military rule, and its politicians have plunged into new democratic system with typical Nigerian spirit; new lawmakers say they are learning about democracy, and how to build it in diverse country of 110 million people; note that executive branch is headed by Pres Olusegun Obasanjo, military ruler in late 1970's; say creation of Senate and House of Representatives are areas where real progress toward democratization has been made; map; photos of Suleiman Isyaku, one of 360 members of Nigeria's new House (M)

1999-09-12.title: To Get Cult To Leave, Japan Cities Buy It Out
1999-09-12.abstract: Growing number of Japanese cities are driving out followers of Aum Shinrikyo religious sect by using public money to buy up property owned by doomsday cult (M)

1999-09-12.title: A New German Assertiveness On Its Foreign Policy Stance
1999-09-12.abstract: Chancellor Gerhard Schroder, marking break with caution of German foreign policy since World War II, has laid out new vision of his country's international role, describing Germany as 'great power in Europe' that will not hestitate to pursue its national interests; new definition of German foreign policy appears to signal formal end of Germany's self-imposed reserve even as it underscores country's irreversible attachment to NATO and European Union; Schroder photo (M)

1999-09-13.title: Malaysian Court Jails Foreign Journalist for Contempt
1999-09-13.abstract: KUALA LUMPUR—  A Canadian journalist has been sentenced by Malaysia's Court of Appeal to six weeks in prison for contempt of court.

1999-09-13.title: M. Ka Dinizulu, Zulu Leader, Dies at 67
1999-09-13.abstract: Mcwayizeni Ka Dinizulu, most senior Prince of Zulu people of South Africa, dies at age 67 after long illness (S)

1999-09-13.title: With Vote, Mexican Right Gives a Hand To Candidate
1999-09-13.abstract: Mexico's National Action Party, pro-business opposition party, selects Vicente Fox Quesada as its presidential candidate in nationwide primary; outcome makes him candidate for governing Institutional Revolutionary Party to beat in 2000 election; polls show he is most popular challenger to governing party (S)

1999-09-13.title: North Korea Said to Agree to End Missile Tests
1999-09-13.abstract: American officials say North Korea has tentatively agreed to end its testing of long-range ballistic missiles in exchange for commitment from US and Japan to move forward with economic assistance (S)

1999-09-13.title: Momcilo Djujic, Serbian Priest and Warrior, Dies at 92
1999-09-13.abstract: Momcilo Djujic, Serbian warrior-priest who fought as Chetnik commander in epic World War II battles in Yugoslavia, dies at age 92 (M)

1999-09-14.title: Aftershock Hits Turkish Cities Struck by Quake in August
1999-09-14.abstract: At least seven people die and hundreds are injured in severe tremor in region of Turkey where more than 15,000 people died in huge earthquake on August 17; tremor was felt in Istanbul, Ankara and Izmir; maps; photo (M)

1999-09-14.title: Clinton Is Ready to Scrap Some North Korea Sanctions
1999-09-14.abstract: Pres Clinton, in move to reward North Korea for tentatively agreeing to stop testing its new long-range missile, prepares to lift some trade sanctions that have barred commerce between both countries for close to 50 years; gesture revives question raised by Congressional critics of Administration's negotiations with North, of whether President is rewarding hard-line Communist Government for defusing crisis it created (M)

1999-09-14.title: NATO Commander Says Armed Serbs Are Slipping Back to Kosovo
1999-09-14.abstract: Gen Wesley K Clark, NATO's Supreme Commander, says groups of Serbian paramilitaries are infiltrating back into Kosovo from Serbia, apparently bent on causing trouble three months after Serbian forces withdrew from province (M)

1999-09-14.title: As Iraqis Starve, U.S. Asserts, Their Leaders Live in Luxury
1999-09-14.abstract: US, which is seeking to force new weapons inspection program on Iraq, reports new evidence that Pres Saddam Hussein has spent money to build 'sprawling amusement park' to entertain his political followers instead of feeding hungry populace; State Dept releases declassified aerial photographs showing complex near Baghdad; photogrqaphs also substantiate reports that Hussein recently bulldozed villages where anti-Govt protests took place (S)

1999-09-14.title: He Criticizes Malaysia for Jailing of Canadian : Clinton Backs Journalist
1999-09-14.abstract: KUALA LUMPUR—  President Bill Clinton said Monday that he was "deeply concerned" about the jailing of a Canadian journalist in Malaysia.

1999-09-15.title: Panel Urges Stepped-Up Attention to Ties With North Korea
1999-09-15.abstract: Presidential advisory panel led by former Defense SecWilliam J Perry has urged Pres Clinton to significantly step up diplomatic and trade ties with North Korea in bid to end North Korean efforts to develop nuclear arms; officials say Clinton has tentatively accepted findings; panel also recommended United States maintain its troop strength in South Korea and remain vigilant to new provocations (M)

1999-09-15.title: Caught in a Backlash to Moscow's Bombings
1999-09-15.abstract: Moscow's thousands of dark-complexioned natives of Caucasus Mountains, Muslim or Christian, have become suspects and potential scapegoats in Russia's reaction to suspected terrorist bombings of two apartment buildings; many are staying at home to avoid bullying by police and neighbors; many Moscow residents fear bombings mean conflict in north Caucasus region of Dagestan, where insurgents seek to create Islamic state, has been extended to city; police begin enforcing Mayor Yuri M Luzhkov's requirement that visitors to city re-register, move aimed at people from Caucasus, many of whom live in city without residency permits; photos (M)

1999-09-15.title: China to Let 50 Slogans Bloom (a Bit, And Just 50)
1999-09-15.abstract: China's Communist Party lists 50 approved slogans for celebration marking People's Republic's 50th birthday; slogans are only messages allowed on posters, banners and lips of celebrants (M)

1999-09-15.title: Ex-P.O.W.'s Sue 5 Big Japanese Companies Over Forced Labor
1999-09-15.abstract: Some former American prisoners of war from World War II have filed class-action suit against five major Japanese corporations, holding they were beaten, starved and forced to work in Japanese factories, mines and shipyards; hold they continue to suffer physical and mental problems; Eli J Warach, lawyer for plaintiffs, says suit names Kawasaki Heavy Industries, Mitsubishi International Corp, Mitsui & Co, Nippon Steel and Showa Denko KK; notes many other Japanese companies used prisoners of war as workers (M)

1999-09-15.title: Protesters Just Say No To 'McDo'; Jospin Glad
1999-09-15.abstract: French Prime Min Lionel Jospin lauds Jose Bove, farmers' union official jailed for leading July demonstration that wrecked McDonald's restaurant building site, television interview; fast-food chain is target of protests over American tariffs on French farm exports imposed because France refuses to import American hormone-treated beef (M)

1999-09-16.title: Respected Economist Struggles to Find Votes in Argentina
1999-09-16.abstract: Domingo Cavallo's third-party presidential race could shake up two parties that have dominated Argentina's politics; former Economy Minister, standing at 9 percent in polls, is poised to win bloc of Congressional seats for his new conservative party, making him swing negotiator in Congress and key player in economic policy; he comments, interview (M)

1999-09-16.title: U.N. Prepares Peacekeepers As Timorese Fight to Survive
1999-09-16.abstract: Tentative calm prevails in Dili, East Timor's capital, after Security Council vote approving deployment of force of up to 8,000 soldiers and equipment offered by more than dozen countries; United Nations emergency expert able to travel out of capital for first time visits largest concentration of refugees remaining in territory; finds them struggling to survive on rice handouts from military (M)

1999-09-16.title: How Schroder's 'Dip' Is Deepening
1999-09-16.abstract: Analysis of German Chancellor Gerhard Schroder's deepening political crisis; central issue is that Germany is running out of money, and proposed deep spending cuts that will affect most Germans are very unpopular; Schroder is trying to change conservative country deeply attached to its habits; his proposed budget, designed to open way for sharp cuts in corporate and personal tax rates, is intended to jolt Germans into rethinking their lives; Schroder is charting course of less state, more personal responsibility, less enveloping social protection, more entrepreneurship; recent election results show public reaction to his plan has been violent; photo (M)

1999-09-16.title: Reporter's Notebook; The Lambasting of Clinton That Didn't Last Long
1999-09-16.abstract: Reporter's Notebook vignettes from Pres Clinton's trip to New Zealand; notes New Zealanders cheered Clinton in spite of his harsh trade restrictions on New Zealand lamb, while China's President Jiang Zemin, also on visit, was booed because of China's policy on Tibet (M)

1999-09-16.title: U.S. and Indonesian Generals Discuss Safety of Troops in Timor
1999-09-16.abstract: Clinton Administration discusses which American forces would take part in peacekeeping mission to East Timor; White House says Gen Joseph W Ralston of Joint Chiefs of Staff has met with Indonesian counterpart seeking to insure full cooperation of Indonesian military (M)

1999-09-17.title: Suicide Note Blames President of Mexico
1999-09-17.abstract: In a last attack on the political system he once served, a former Mexican justice official who apparently killed himself on Wednesday rather than face money-laundering charges in the United States blamed Mexican President Ernesto Zedillo for both his own death and the 1994 murder of his brother.    In a suicide note made public by his widow, Mario Ruiz Massieu charged that Mr. Zedillo had never forgiven his criticism of party officials after his brother, a party leader, was killed. He said the evidence against him was fabricated.

1999-09-17.title: Albright Warns the Russians to Battle Corruption, or Else
1999-09-17.abstract: Secretary of State Madeleine K Albright criticizes Russia's leaders for failing to combat corruption, but defends Clinton Administration policy of supporting aid to Russia; Albright makes some of Administration's sharpest remarks since international investigation of money-laundering allegations surfaced; but her remarks do little to calm criticism in Congress because, for time being, she stops short of threatening to withdraw support for loans from International Monetary Fund; furor in Congress over Russian scandal makes it clear Republicans intend to make it campaign issue in 2000 (M)

1999-09-17.title: World Briefing
1999-09-17.abstract: ASIA    MYANMAR: 2D BRITON JAILED -- A court sentenced a British democracy activist, Rachel Goldwyn, to seven years in prison at labor for protesting against Myanmar's military Government. Ms. Goldwyn, 28, of London, looked saddened by the sentence, the maximum possible. She was arrested after chaining herself to a lamppost in Yangon on Sept. 7 and shouting pro-democracy slogans. Another Briton, James Mawdsley, who was jailed for 17 years for anti-Government protests on Sept. 2, was allowed his first visit by a British consular official.   (AP, Agence France-Presse)

1999-09-17.title: Caracas Journal; It's Sunday, and Venezuelans Have the Leader's Ear
1999-09-17.abstract: Venezuela's most unusual radio show is Hello, President, featuring Pres Hugo Chavez taking calls every Sunday morning for two hours from any Venezuelan with a problem, a complaint or a suggestion; Chavez also makes point of discussing his official agenda for coming week, often announcing major policy initiatives on the show; program exemplifies Chavez's notions of what constitutes democracy; he has repeatedly attacked Venezuela's National Congress and state legislatures as unrepresentative bodies out of touch with citizenry; photo (M)

1999-09-17.title: Timor Peacekeeping Force Gathers in Australia
1999-09-17.abstract: Foreign troops, ships and planes converge in Darwin, Australia, as international peacekeeping mission prepares to depart for East Timor; soldiers from Britain, Thailand and nine other countries, including small contingent of Americans, will join 4,500 Australian troops (M)

1999-09-18.title: Tokyo Journal; Still Smiling, New Geishas Pour Sake and Charm
1999-09-18.abstract: Geishas, dying breed in Japan, are being replaced by kimono-clad hostesses called furisode-san; photo (M)

1999-09-18.title: Mexico City's Bank Robbers Feel Police Chief's Iron Hand
1999-09-18.abstract: Police Chief Alejandro Gertz Manero notes that crime in Mexico City has risen to record levels but bank robberies have fallen (M)

1999-09-18.title: TRADE SANCTIONS ON NORTH KOREA ARE EASED BY U.S.
1999-09-18.abstract: Pres Clinton orders easing of economic sanctions against North Korea in what officials say is first step in long-term plan to induce North to abandon its missile and nuclear programs; announcement--most extensive relaxation of sanctions against North since Korean War--comes four days after North Korea agreed to temporary halt in its tests of long-range missiles; photo (M)

1999-09-18.title: Arafat Slips Across Border For Secret Talks With Barak
1999-09-18.abstract: Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat sneaks into Israel to meet with Prime Min Ehud Barak and discuss negotiations for permanent peace (M)

1999-09-18.title: Kosovo Rebels' Political Chief Calls for U.N. Representation
1999-09-18.abstract: Hashim Thaci, political leader of Kosovo Liberation Army, says that Kosovo Albanians want to be represented at United Nations; photo (M)û

1999-09-19.title: A Small Flotilla Sets Sail For Mission in East Timor
1999-09-19.abstract: a small flotilla sets sail for mission in east

1999-09-19.title: U.N.'s Workers Become Targets In Angry Lands
1999-09-19.abstract: United Nations says its relief workers, once protected by their status as neutral do-gooders, have increasingly become targets of violence in troubled lands; says 180 UN workers have been killed on duty since 1992, including at least 141 locally recruited staff members; attacks on UN workers are now used to attract media attention and send political messages (M)l

1999-09-19.title: A Bully Pulpit to Restore Lost African Icons
Burmese Pain In Spotlight As 2 Britons Sent to Prison
1999-09-19.abstract: Rachel Goldwyn is sentenced to seven years at hard labor in Myanmar for staging one-person protest against military Government; is second young Briton in two weeks to be harshly sentenced, drawing concern of family members and diplomats and further attention to political repression in former Burma; map (M)

1999-09-19.title: Khat-Chewing Yemen Told to Break Ancient Habit
1999-09-19.abstract: Pres Ali Abdullah Saleh has begun drive to stamp out use of khat, mild narcotic leaf chewed by some 80 percent of Yemeni men and 60 percent of women; urges Yemenis to devote khat-chewing time to sports or learning; most Yeminis dismiss suggestion; map; photos (M)

1999-09-20.title: How to Celebrate the Millennium in Good Company : It's a Date, but Where's the Party?
1999-09-20.abstract: PARIS—  Do you fancy digesting the millennium's arrival at the celebrated Tour d'Argent restaurant, gazing down on the living time-capsule Notre Dame cathedral, far from hoi polloi and ceremonial musak? The most intrusive sound will be the rich swish of caviar and vintage crus down elegant gullets. Sound good?

1999-09-20.title: As Talks Snag, NATO Puts Off Disarming Kosovo Rebels; Dispute Is Over Guns
1999-09-20.abstract: Kosovo Liberation Army misses midnight deadline for its final disarmament and demobilization as its commanders wrangle with NATO officials over number of weapons its men will be allowed to retain in a civilian force; map (M)

1999-09-20.title: Officials Say Deployment Seeks to Offset Dominant Role by Australia : Asians Move to Join UN Timor Force
1999-09-20.abstract: SINGAPORE—  Alarmed at the rise of xenophobic nationalism in Indonesia over its failure to control East Timor, Asian nations are rushing military personnel to Darwin, Australia, so that they can join the multinational force that is expected to start landing in the territory Monday.

1999-09-20.title: New Spy Accusations Captivate British Press
1999-09-20.abstract: Four Britons are identified as suspected Communist agents, bringing to eight the number of people recently acused of being cold war informers and prompting calls on Government to clarify why there have been no prosecutions and whether British security has been undermined; Robin Pearson, postgraduate studies director at Hull University, is identified as informer for East Germany; Vic Allen, retired economics professor at Leeds University, is said to have passed information about Campaign for Nuclear Disarmament to East Germany; Dr Gwyneth Edwards, German-language lecturer at Loughborough University, is accused of having collaborated with East German secret police, the Stasi; Richard Clements, former editor of leftist weekly Tribune and adviser to former Labor Party leader Neil Kinnock, is identified as agent for former Soviet Union; last week, accusations were brought against former Labor members of Parliament Tom Briberg and Raymond Fletcher, former Scotland Yard officer John Symonds, and a suburban London woman, Melita Norwood; unmasking of supposed spies is receiving enormous attention in British press (M)

1999-09-20.title: Australian Forces Reach East Timor To Aid in Recovery
1999-09-20.abstract: First units of international peacekeeping force, a small contingent of Australians, land in East Timor; they are to be followed by 2,500 soldiers aboard nine warships from Australia, Great Britain and New Zealand gathered offshore; force is expected to grow to about 8,000; mission commander, Maj Gen Peter Cosgrove of Australia, says troops encountered cordial reception by Indonesian military; maps; photos (M)

1999-09-21.title: Timor Death Toll Disputed
1999-09-21.abstract: Indonesia's top military commander, Gen Wiranto, disputes reports of thousands of deaths in East Timor; claims fewer than 100 people have been killed there (S)

1999-09-21.title: U.N. PEACEKEEPERS STAKE TIMOR CLAIM
1999-09-21.abstract: Vanguard of international peacekeeping force stakes its claim to silent and broken town of Dili in East Timor under gaze of clusters of refugees, sullen Indonesian troops and small, angry groups of militiamen; more than 1,000 Australian soldiers spread out through burned, looted and evacuated city; there is no resistance, but three large fires are set hours after they arrive; photo (M)

1999-09-21.title: Iraq Rejects Several Proposals For New Weapon Inspections
1999-09-21.abstract: Pres Saddam Hussein of Iraq indicates he is unwilling to go along with even most generous of several proposals aimed at establishing new United Nations inspection system; this is fresh blow to prospect of any swift return of weapons inspectors to Iraq's soil; Hussein's tough stance complicates efforts of United Nations Security Council's five permanent members to agree on plan to entice Iraq to allow renewed inspections of its weapons programs (M)

1999-09-21.title: WORLD BRIEFING
1999-09-21.abstract: THE AMERICAS    MEXICO: EXTRADITION BY SWISS -- Authorities in Switzerland have agreed to extradite to Mexico the former chief executive of the holding company that owns Aeromexico and Mexicana Airlines, Gerardo de Prevoisin. Mr. de Prevoisin fled into exile in 1994 as the airlines he bought from the Government in 1988 careered toward bankruptcy, and he was later charged with defrauding the companies of $57.8 million. He has said he donated part of the money to President Ernesto Zedillo's 1994 election campaign.   Sam Dillon (NYT)    COLOMBIA: BISHOP FREED -- A left-wing guerrilla group that kidnapped a Roman Catholic bishop more than a month ago has released the cleric, but is demanding that the Vatican transfer him and another bishop in the region to other dioceses. ''I will not go,'' was the response of the kidnapped bishop, Msgr. Jose de Jesus Quintero, said by the People's Liberation Army to favor right-wing paramilitary groups that have recently become active in the area.   Larry Rohter (NYT)

1999-09-21.title: Bank Affair and a Yeltsin Son-in-Law
1999-09-21.abstract: Clinton Administration officials say Federal investigation of money laundering at Bank of New York is examining transactions with bank by people close to Pres Boris N Yeltsin of Russia; Federal Bureau of Investigation has reportedly found transactions involving Pavel P Borodin, head of Yeltsin's property management team, and Leonid Dyachenko, influential political adviser to Yeltsin who is also Yeltsin's son-in-law; Yeltsin's spokesman has previously claimed that neither Yeltsin nor any members of immediate Yeltsin family has foreign bank account; spokesman now says disclaimer did not apply to Yeltsin's in-laws; Borodin has repeatedly denied any wrongdoing, and dismissed allegations against him as politically motivated (M)

1999-09-22.title: Kosovo Rebel Leader Basking In Warmth of Deal's Reception
1999-09-22.abstract: NATO and United Nations officials release details about force that will replace rebel Kosovo Liberation Army, as guerrillas quietly congratulate themselves on winning deal they wanted; Kosovo Protection Corps will have 3,000 active and 2,000 reserve members; it has been portrayed as civilian by Western officials, but rebels consider it defense force (M)

1999-09-22.title: Rescuers Sift Rubble of Taiwan's Cities
1999-09-22.abstract: Over 1,700 people are reported dead and at least 4,400 injured in earthquake that devastated Taiwan on Sept 21; Interior Ministry says at least 30,000 homes have been destroyed; rescuers are trying to reach thousands of people still trapped inside toppled buildings; highways and bridges are impassable to traffic in worst-hit counties near epicenter, Taichung and Nantou; much of island, including sections of Taipei, are in darkness because of power failures; over 1,000 aftershocks are recorded, including one that registers potent 6.8; main quake measured 7.7 and was centered 90 miles south of Taipei; map; photos (M)

1999-09-22.title: European Allies to Spend More on Weapons
1999-09-22.abstract: NATO defense ministers, meeting in Toronto, Canada, for first time since end of Kosovo air war, agree that European members of alliance have to spend far more money on modern arms and equipment to carry their share of responsibility in war (M)

1999-09-22.title: East Timorese, First Wary, Then Jubilant, Greet U.N. Troops in Village Near Capital
1999-09-22.abstract: United Nations officials say over 2,000 peackeeping troops have arrived in East Timor, most of them from Australia; say they will reach 3,000 by Sept 22, building toward final target of 8,000; map; photo (M)

1999-09-22.title: Raise Intervention Abilities, Clinton Urges U.N. Members
1999-09-22.abstract: Pres Clinton, in speech to General Assembly, urges other members of United Nations to strengthen their collective capacity for intervening to prevent kinds of mass killings and ethnic repression that ravaged Kosovo and East Timor, saying that there are limits to what individual nations can accomplish; photo (M)

1999-09-23.title: Fewer at Anti-Milosevic Rallies, but Leaders Vow to Continue
1999-09-23.abstract: Street protests intended to oust Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic appear to falter in Belgrade and other Serbian cities on second day, giving some comfort to Government deeply shaken by loss of Kosovo (M)

1999-09-23.title: Aid to Timor Delayed by Dangers in Its Terrified Streets
1999-09-23.abstract: Concerns about security are delaying start of aid program to help hundreds of thousands of East Timorese refugees in urgent need of food, shelter and medical care; military officials say East Timor remains tense and unpredictable on fourth day of international peacekeeping mission to subdue terrorism; map; photo (M)

1999-09-23.title: Families of Taiwan's Missing See Little Hope in the Rubble
1999-09-23.abstract: Article on scene in Tali, Taiwan, following Sept 20 earthquake describes efforts to rescue 39 people trapped in rubble of 13-story apartment building; notes that no one knows if any are still alive (M)

1999-09-23.title: The Lowest Castes in India, Reaching for Power, Shake Up the System
1999-09-23.abstract: Growing political power and independence of lowest castes is being felt throughout India; surveys show they are voting in greater proportions than ever before, greater even than upper castes; democracy is leading to greater social equality for people who had been excluded from political power for first four decades of Indian independence; map; photo (M)

1999-09-23.title: China and Others Reject Pleas That U.N. Intervene in Civil Wars
1999-09-23.abstract: Sec Gen Kofi Annan's call for more active intervention by United Nations when civilian populations are at risk is meeting resistance in General Assembly from China and small but vocal group of nations; China's Foreign Min Tang Jiaxuan says respect for national sovereignty and noninterference are 'basic principles governing international relations' (M)

1999-09-24.title: State Dept. Expert Upbeat About Russian Fund Case
1999-09-24.abstract: Deputy State Sec Strobe Talbott, Pres Clinton's top Russia expert, testifies before Senate Foreign Relations Committee on corruption in Russia; gives unusually upbeat assessment of widening investigation into allegations of Russian laundering of billions of dollars through American banks and whether top Administration officials turned blind eye to corruption at highest levels of Russian Government; photo (M)

1999-09-24.title: London Law Firm Is Target Of Russia Banking Inquiry
1999-09-24.abstract: Manhattan District Attorney's Office is looking into now-defunct London law firm, Talbott, Creggy & Company, for clues to genesis of Russian money movements through Bank of New York that are now under Federal investigation (M)

1999-09-24.title: Russia Bombs Chechnya Sites; Major Step-Up
1999-09-24.abstract: Russian warplanes bomb Grozny airport in Chechnya and set oil refinery ablaze on outskirts of city, as Kremlin takes its military campaign against Islamic rebels deep into Chechen territory; Russian bombing raids near Chechnya's capital represent a major stepping-up of fighting and signals change of strategy; maps (M)

1999-09-24.title: Basil Rodzianko, 84, Bishop in Orthodox Church
1999-09-24.abstract: Bishop Basil Rodzianko, retired Bishop of Orthodox Church in America who was known for broadcasts oni religious topics that he made from Britain and US to what is now Russia for over three decades, dies at age 84 (M)

1999-09-24.title: Taiwan Begins Looking at Timetable and Cost to Rebuild
1999-09-24.abstract: Taiwan Government officials say that destruction from earthquake and its aftershocks will significantly reduce Taiwan's economic growth for 1999; warn that repairing the many damaged buildings, highways, power generators and other structures could take years; latest Government tally shows more than 2,100 people were killed and at least 7,800 injured; photo (M)

1999-09-25.title: Ex-Premier Andreotti Acquitted of Mafia Murder Conspiracy
1999-09-25.abstract: Giulio Andreotti, seven-time Italian Prime Minister, is acquitted of conspiring with Mafia in 1979 murder of Carmine Pecorelli, a muckracking journalist, and also of killing him; so are four other co-defendants, some of whom have been convicted of mob-related crimes in other cases; verdict revives soul-searching about country's judicial system and prosecutors' reliance on testimony of turncoat informants (M)

1999-09-25.title: Third in Mexican Polls, a Leftist Campaigner Manages to Project Optimism
1999-09-25.abstract: Cuauhtemoc Cardenas Solorzano, who is stepping down as mayor of Mexico City to run for President, is undaunted by newspaper poll showing that far more city resident disapprove of his performance in office than approve; is also undaunted by polls in Presidential race showing him running far behind either of two most likely candidates of ruling Institutional Revolutionary Party and the leading opposition candidate, Vicente Fox Quesada; photo (M)

1999-09-25.title: Re-entry to Index Could Lure Back Funds : Is Malaysia a Safe Bet?
1999-09-25.abstract: KUALA LUMPUR—  In a perfect world, stock market investors would pick companies purely on the basis of economic fundamentals.

1999-09-25.title: Jakarta Delays Security Bill as Riots Rage
1999-09-25.abstract: Pres B J Habibie of Indonesia says he will delay signing security law whose passage on Sept 23 prompted rioting by students; they object both to content of law, which they say would grant sweeping new powers to the military to impose martial law, and to the way it was rushed through Parliament; clashes between police and student rage all night, and the mayhem intensifies as troops fight thousands of students and others in heart of Jakarta's business district; photo (M)

1999-09-25.title: France Shops for Another Pretty Face
1999-09-25.abstract: France's mayors vote on a model for a new Marianne, the national symbol, choosing from five beautiful finalists, including two supermodels; critics ask why a woman of intellect and accomplishments rather than her looks could not be Marianne; the new Marianne will join earlier versions from which each mayor selects the statue that will grace his or her city hall; photos (M)

1999-09-26.title: Champagne Makers Promise Plenty to Cry Into on Dec. 31
1999-09-26.abstract: Sales of French Champagne are setting records, as Americans prepare to ring in the millennium; winemakers in heart of Champagne-making region of France laugh off talk of shortages; several years ago, Champagne houses and growers of three grapes essential for genuine Champagne agreed to produce enough grapes to make average of 270 million bottles a year for rest of 1990's, about 50 million bottles more than they had been selling in recession years; producers also agreed to draw down equivalent of 132 million bottles of reserve wine, normally saved for years with poor harvest, to use it to put more Champagne on market in 1999 than ever before; result is that there will be plenty of nonvintage wine to go around; Champagne sales this year will likely surpass 300 million bottles and set record, up to 15 percent more than last year's figures; photos; graph; map (M)

1999-09-26.title: Debate Over Russia
1999-09-26.abstract: Analysis of Congressional probe into allegations of Russian corruption and money laundering at Bank of New York; says two things hampered Republicans hoping to reap political hay from scandal: fact that House Banking Committee's chairman, Rep James A Leach, is thoughtful moderate who rejected premise of Reps Dick Armey and Tom Delay about Clinton Administration 'losing' Russia, and that Administration's sharpest foreign policy critic in Senate, Jesse Helms, was mostly quiet about matter (M)

1999-09-26.title: India's Election Marathon Lumbers Into Final Week
1999-09-26.abstract: India enters its fourth and final week of marathon national elections, massive effort to make sure every citizen--no matter how remote--gets chance to vote; election officials expect more than 350 million people to vote, 60 percent of all eligible adults; political analysts expect Hindu nationalist-led alliance to still be in power, but probabaly backed this time by more powerful collection of 12 parties and probably commanding stronger majority than it did in April before it lost confidence of Parliament by single vote; Prime Min Atal Behari Vajpayee seems certain to head new Government, as he did old one; photo (M)

1999-09-26.title: Vote for Me, Declares Argentine, I'm Boring
1999-09-26.abstract: Mayor Fernando de la Rua of Buenos Aires, running for president of Argentina, takes every opportunity to contrast himself with Pres Carlos Saul Menem and to tie his principal opponent, Buenos Aires Gov Eduardo Duhalde, to departing President and his glitzy style of politics; de la Rua's campaign offers few detailed proposals, but blandness appears to be winning message at time of mounting Government scandals, street crime and unemployment; de la Rua has gaping lead in polls with just month to go before Oct 24 vote (M)

1999-09-26.title: Shock and Sorrow in the Aftermath of Timor's Violence
1999-09-26.abstract: Countless people in towns around East Timor are victims of rampage by violent men who burned thousands of homes and kidnapped tens of thousands of people in mass deportation of one-fourth of population; international peacekeeping forces have arrived, and in Dili, mood has shifted from one of terror to one of anxiety and pain of personal traumas; refugees walk back to their old neighborhoods to see whether their homes are still standing and whether it is safe to return to live in them; photo (M)

1999-09-27.title: JAPAN GIVES HINT THAT IT WILL ACT TO HALT YEN'S RISE
1999-09-27.abstract: Bank of Japan's governor hints he may reverse course and act to halt three-month-long rise in value of Japanese yen before it chokes off country's fragile economic recovery and provokes further clashes with United States; vaguely worded announcement from Masaru Hayami, plus communique issued by finance ministers of Group of 7 largest industrialized nations, is enough to strengthen dollar slightly and weaken yen; just last week Hayami and central bank defied Japanese politicians and Washington by refusing to stop rise in yen's value; Hayami's comments are important in part because Wall Street reacted badly to moves last week by Bank of Japan; future of American stock markets seem increasingly dependent on decisions of Japan's central bank, institution that operates by its own rules and market rationale (M)

1999-09-27.title: Serbs Rally, Wearily Looking for Someone Else
1999-09-27.abstract: Tens of thousands of people, young and old, turn out for Serbia's first week of rallies to try to oust Pres Slobodan Milosevic, but turnout is not even close to politically definitive figure in country of 11 million people; behavior of protesters is extraordinarily polite; people are tired and afraid of losing what little is left to them, and fearful of what will happen to them for their participation in rallies; indisputable fact about opposition is its division, in matter of strategy, goals and personalities; photo (M)

1999-09-27.title: With British Court Hearings Set, Pinochet Will Soon Know Fate
1999-09-27.abstract: Gen Augusto Pinochet, held in Britain under house arrest for nearly year, will soon learn if he must go to Spain to face charges of crimes against humanity in connection with deaths and disappearances of more than 3,000 people dating back to 1973, year he sized power in Chile; whatever the outcome, Pinochet case has secured valuable legal precedent for human rights movement: finding that former heads of state do not enjoy immunity from prosecution for systematic atrocities; case has caused strain in relations between Spain and Chile, which intensified this month when Chile recalled its Ambassador to Madrid for second time to protest over treatment of Pinochet (M)

1999-09-27.title: Russia Hotly Protests Audits Demanded by I.M.F. for Loans
1999-09-27.abstract: Viktor Gerashchenko, head of Russia's central bank, makes first open and harsh criticism of extensive new auditing demanded by Washington before lending new money to Moscow through International Monetary Fund; suggests Russia would resist intensive audits of its books; American officials discount Gerashchenko's outburst, noting it was in sharp contrast to his behavior during meeting with Treasury Secretary Lawrence H Summers; Americans suggest that Gerashchenko may have been playing to domestic audience in Russia, where accusations of money laundering and demands for new audits smack of more American interference in running of Russia' chaotic economy (M)

1999-09-27.title: Chechens Flee Russian Bombs By Thousands
1999-09-27.abstract: Thousands of desperate Chechen refugees flee after days of intensive air bombardment in Russia's war against Islamic militants; more than 100,000 of them have crossed border into neighboring Russian republic of Ingushetia, prompting Ingush authorities to appeal to Moscow for emergency assistance, including food and tents; Akhmed Malsagov, acting Prime Minister of Ingushetia, closes border, saying his republic is on brink of humanitarian disaster; situation remains chaotic as 10-mile-long stretch of major highway is clogged with lines of cars and pedestrians trying to make their way across border; Russia's Defense Minister Marshal Igor Sergeyev hints about sending ground troops into Chechnya to go after rebel sanctuaries; photo; map (M)

1999-09-28.title: She Knows Species Like Family Members
1999-09-28.abstract: Biographical profile of Lu Zhi, environmentalist with World Wide Fund for Nature and leading light in China's nascent conservation movement who may know more than anyone about giant pandas; photo (M)

1999-09-28.title: He Wants Only to Entertain, With Quality
1999-09-28.abstract: Biographical profile of Sun Jianjun, 43-year-old producer of classical music concerts, television programs and movies in China; photo (M)

1999-09-28.title: TV Producer Is From New Breed of Woman
1999-09-28.abstract: Biographical profile of Zhao Shujing, executive producer of China's most influential television show for women, Half the Sky; photo (M)

1999-09-28.title: Tight Security Blitz Aims to Prevent Protests in Beijing
1999-09-28.abstract: Chinese authorities are focusing on security as they complete preparations for festivities on Oct 1 marking 50th anniversary of Communist rule (M)

1999-09-28.title: Imitating NATO: A Script Is Adapted for Chechnya
1999-09-28.abstract: Michael R Gordon analysis notes that Russian military leaders, in explaining bombing of Chechnya, say they are copying NATO's air campaign against Yugoslavia; maintains that there are enormous differences between Yugoslavia and Chechnya; says Chechnya lacks effective central government that could bring to heel Islamic militants Moscow is fighting; says bombing strikes may plunge already lawless region into utter chaos and win new recruits for militants Moscow wants to neutralize; map; photo (M)

1999-09-29.title: Peacekeepers Swoop Down to Aid a Town in East Timor
1999-09-29.abstract: Multinational peacekeeping force moves into Liquica, East Timor, in opening of campaign to extend its reach beyond capital of Dili, and to subdue armed resistance throughout territory; map; photo (M)

1999-09-29.title: U.S. Likely to Regain a U.N. Finance Seat
1999-09-29.abstract: Senior American official says US is likely to regain seat on important United Nations budget committee, clearing one hurdle Clinton Administration faces in getting Congress to pay back dues to organization (M)

1999-09-29.title: Yeltsin Foes Urge an End To Campaign In Chechnya
1999-09-29.abstract: Several leading Russian opposition politicians call for end to six days of Russian bombing in Chechnya as toll continues to mount; 384 people reportedly have been killed and over 1,000 wounded in raids; map; photo (M)

1999-09-29.title: Jerusalem Journal; Succoth in Israel, and Here Come the Evangelicals!
1999-09-29.abstract: Five thousand members of International Christian Embassy, evangelical group that espouses Zionist cause, march in Jerusalem for feast of Succoth; photo (M)

1999-09-29.title: RULING PARTY GETS A LIFT IN MEXICO AS FOES DISAGREE
1999-09-29.abstract: Talks among opposition parties to forge coalition that would back single presidential candidate collapse, increasing chances that Institutional Revolutionary Party, which has controlled Mexico for seven decades, will win power once again next year; breakdown comes after four months of negotiations between right-of-center National Action Party, known as PAN, and other main opposition group, left-leaning Party of Democratic Revolution; PAN and its presidential candidate, Vicente Fox Quesada, reject coalition; two parties cannot agree on how to choose between Fox and Cuauhtemoc Cardenas Solorzano, leader of leftist party; Cardenas resigns as Mayor of Mexico City to begin his third presidential bid; photo (M)

1999-09-30.title: Gore, Playing Underdog, Wants Bradley to Debate
1999-09-30.abstract: WASHINGTON—  Vice President Al Gore, in a tacit but surprising admission that his campaign for the Democratic presidential nomination was stumbling, on Wednesday challenged his fast-rising rival, former Senator Bill Bradley, to a series of debates.

1999-09-30.title: Russian Ex-Premier, in the U.S., Says Corruption Is Exaggerated
1999-09-30.abstract: Former Russian Prime Min Viktor S Chernomyrdin, visiting Washington, DC, says that accusations of Russian corruption and money-laundering have poisoned relations with US, and he urges political and business leaders in both countries to help keep investigation from derailing larger strategic relationship (M)

1999-09-30.title: U.N. Is Ready to Consider Sending Peacekeepers to Sierra Leone
1999-09-30.abstract: Security Council is considering whether to dispatch peacekeepers to Sierra Leone; British draft resolution proposes sending peacekeepers for initial period of 12 months; Sec Gen Kofi Annan recommends that 6,000 soldiers be deployed in Sierra Leone, which has been ravaged by years of civil war (M)

1999-09-30.title: World Briefing
1999-09-30.abstract: EUROPE    BRITAIN: PINOCHET DEFENSE  Many of the interrogation techniques used during Gen. Augusto Pinochet's dictatorship in Chile were the same used by British forces in Northern Ireland and found by the European Court of Human Rights not to constitute torture, the lawyer leading the general's defense told a London court. Spain is seeking the extradition of Mr. Pinochet, 83, to stand trial on torture charges.    Warren Hoge (NYT)

1999-09-30.title: Canada Is Taking a Tougher Line With Illegal Chinese Migrants
1999-09-30.abstract: Canada is taking harder line against illegal Chinese immigrants; officials say that four trawlers were confiscated and 560 undocumented Chinese citizens detained this summer (M)
